structure.title: Introduction
structure.summary: PageIndex is a revolutionary approach to document retrieval that uses hierarchical tree structures and LLM reasoning instead of traditional vector embeddings. This approach offers several advantages: No vector database required Interpretable search process Better handling of document structure Reduced storage requirements

structure.title: Installation
structure.summary: eIndex in just a few line

structure.title: Quick Start
structure.summary: Get started with PageIndex in just a few lines of code: import { PageIndex } from 'pageindex-ts'; const pageIndex = new PageIndex({ openaiApiKey: process.env.OPENAIAPIKEY }); // Index a document const doc = await pageIndex.indexFromFile('document.pdf'); // Search the document const results = await pageIndex.query('What is the main topic?');

structure.title: API Reference
structure.summary: PageIndex Class The main class for document indexing and querying. Constructor new PageIndex(config: PageIndexConfig) Parameter Type Description openaiApiKey string Your OpenAI API key openaiModel string Model to use (default: gpt-4o-mini) dataDir string Directory for storing indexes Indexing Methods indexFromFile(filePath, options) Index a document from a local file path.

nodes.title: PageIndex Class
nodes.summary: The main class for document indexing and querying.

nodes.title: Constructor
nodes.summary: new PageIndex(config: PageIndexConfig) Parameter Type Description openaiApiKey string Your OpenAI API key openaiModel string Model to use (default: gpt-4o-mini) dataDir string Directory for storing indexes

nodes.title: Indexing Methods
nodes.summary: ch - Queries are answered by traversing the tree w

nodes.title: indexFromFile(filePath, options)
nodes.summary: Index a document from a local file path. const doc = await pageIndex.indexFromFile('/path/to/document.pdf', { name: 'my-document', useLLMTreeBuilder: true });

nodes.title: indexFromUrl(url, options)
nodes.summary: Index a document from a URL. const doc = await pageIndex.indexFromUrl('https://example.com/doc.pdf');

nodes.title: indexContent(content, name, contentType)
nodes.summary: Index content directly. const doc = await pageIndex.indexContent(buffer, 'doc-name', 'text/markdown');

nodes.title: Search Methods
nodes.summary: ment List  const documents = await pageIndex

nodes.title: query(query, documentIds, options)
nodes.summary: Search indexed documents using LLM reasoning. const results = await pageIndex.query( 'What are the key features?', ['doc-id-1', 'doc-id-2'], { topK: 5, includeReasoning: true } );

nodes.title: SearchOptions
nodes.summary: Option Type Default Description topK number 3 Number of results to return maxIterations number 5 Max tree traversal iterations includeReasoning boolean true Include LLM reasoning in results

structure.title: Supported Document Formats
structure.summary: PDF - Portable Document Format (.pdf) HTML - HyperText Markup Language (.html, .htm) Markdown - Markdown files (.md) Plain Text - Text files (.txt)

structure.title: Architecture
structure.summary: PageIndex uses a three-stage process: Parsing - Documents are parsed into structured sections Tree Building - Sections are organized into a hierarchical tree LLM Search - Queries are answered by traversing the tree with LLM reasoning

nodes.title: Tree Structure
nodes.summary: Each node in the tree contains: nodeid - Unique identifier title - Section title summary - Content summary startindex - Starting page/position endindex - Ending page/position nodes - Child nodes

nodes.title: Index Multiple Documents
nodes.summary: const docs = ['doc1.pdf', 'doc2.md', 'doc3.html']; for (const doc of docs) { await pageIndex.indexFromFile(doc); }
